content.verdict: Six Senses Punakha has the most photographed entrance in Bhutan — a covered flying bridge that arcs over a ravine into a hillside lodge with an infinity pool that appears to spill onto the terraced rice paddies below. With 22 suites in the warm sub-tropical Punakha valley, it's the most picture-perfect of the Six Senses Bhutan circuit, and a fixture on honeymoon shortlists.
content.best_room: The Suite with bukhari woodstove and a private balcony over the rice fields is the romantic choice — book one of the Suites at the end of the row for the most direct paddy views. The Two-Bedroom Villa is the splurge for a full private retreat.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival via flying bridge
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive from Thimphu over the 3,100m Dochula Pass to the warmer Punakha valley. Cross the iconic flying bridge into the lodge. Welcome tea, in-suite hot stone bath, dinner overlooking the paddies.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Punakha Dzong
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit Punakha Dzong, Bhutan's most beautiful fortress at the river confluence. The Royal Wedding chapel is a moving stop for newlyweds. Picnic on the riverbank. Sunset infinity pool with the rice fields lit golden.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Khamsum Yulley Namgyal Chorten
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hike across rice paddies and a footbridge to this hilltop four-story chorten built by the queen mother. Sunset prayer flag tying ceremony — leave a flag for your marriage.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mo Chhu rafting and Chimi Lhakhang
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day gentle rafting down the Mo Chhu past prayer flags and the dzong. Afternoon walk through fields to Chimi Lhakhang, the Divine Madman's fertility temple.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wellness day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Sunrise yoga overlooking the paddies, Six Senses signature couples massage, long hot stone bath. Afternoon at the organic farm picking ingredients for dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to Phobjikha
itinerary_7_nights.description: Optional half-day drive to the Phobjikha valley for the black-necked cranes (Nov-Mar) and Gangtey Goemba monastery. Or stay in for a slow day by the pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on your suite balcony, drive back over Dochula Pass to Paro airport for the international departure.

caveats: Bhutan visa requires a licensed tour operator — Six Senses handles this; allow 4-6 weeks.
Sustainable Development Fee of $200 per person per night applies.
Punakha is sub-tropical and warm year-round — bring layers for the higher passes you'll cross to get here.
Internet is available but intentionally slow — part of the Six Senses philosophy.

faqs.question: Is Six Senses Punakha good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It's one of the most photogenic honeymoon stays in the Himalayas — the flying-bridge entrance, infinity pool over rice paddies, and Chimi Lhakhang fertility temple nearby make it a romantic standout.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: October-March for the warm sub-tropical climate at lower elevation. April-May for spring blossoms. Monsoon June-August brings leeches and rain.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.answer: Four to six months ahead. Multi-lodge Six Senses Bhutan circuits (Paro, Punakha, Thimphu, Gangtey, Bumthang) book early.

faqs.question: Do we need a guide?
faqs.answer: Yes — required by Bhutanese tourism law and included in the Six Senses package.

faqs.question: What's the best room type?
faqs.answer: End-of-row Suites for direct paddy views. The Two-Bedroom Villa for a fully private splurge.

faqs.question: How do we get to Six Senses Punakha?
faqs.answer: Fly into Paro, then drive 3.5 hours via Thimphu and Dochula Pass. Six Senses handles all transfers.
